item.title: Trio Charged With Bay Roberts Murder Appear in Court Via Video
item.dc:creator: Brian Callahan
item.description:
Well, by the looks of it, we’ve got quite the tale coming out of Harbour Grace. A couple of fellas and a lady are up in court for murderin’ a bloke from Spaniard’s Bay. The poor 42-year-old lad had his name kept out of the news, but they say he wound up in a right state—found in a burnt-up pickup. Apparently, these three geniuses thought torching the truck was a good way to deal with the remains.
We’ve got Matthew Kavanagh and Stephanie Noftall from Harbour Grace and Terry Cole from Carbonear all charged with first-degree murder. Oh, and there’s some fuss about a Honda Civic they reckon was involved, but I’m no car expert—so unless it’s got a pair of oars, I’m not the fella to ask about that.
Seems like when the cops went to nab them, they had half the town on edge, telling folks to stay inside until they talked the suspects out of their house without any trouble. They’re in the slammer in St. John’s and Clarenville for now, appearing in court today by video. Not much happened other than assigning them legal aid and telling them not to chat with each other or some witnesses.
Everything's still in the early go, with the Crown waiting for some paperwork from the police. They’re set to give us more drama come November 5th. There’s nothing like a bit of intrigue to warm your bones this fall, eh? Wonder what the eejits were thinking—like something outta a bad TV show!
item.content:encoded:
Well, by the looks of it, we’ve got quite the tale coming out of Harbour Grace. A couple of fellas and a lady are up in court for murderin’ a bloke from Spaniard’s Bay. The poor 42-year-old lad had his name kept out of the news, but they say he wound up in a right state—found in a burnt-up pickup. Apparently, these three geniuses thought torching the truck was a good way to deal with the remains.
We’ve got Matthew Kavanagh and Stephanie Noftall from Harbour Grace and Terry Cole from Carbonear all charged with first-degree murder. Oh, and there’s some fuss about a Honda Civic they reckon was involved, but I’m no car expert—so unless it’s got a pair of oars, I’m not the fella to ask about that.
Seems like when the cops went to nab them, they had half the town on edge, telling folks to stay inside until they talked the suspects out of their house without any trouble. They’re in the slammer in St. John’s and Clarenville for now, appearing in court today by video. Not much happened other than assigning them legal aid and telling them not to chat with each other or some witnesses.
Everything's still in the early go, with the Crown waiting for some paperwork from the police. They’re set to give us more drama come November 5th. There’s nothing like a bit of intrigue to warm your bones this fall, eh? Wonder what the eejits were thinking—like something outta a bad TV show!

item.title: Man Driving Moped Dies Following Collision with Backhoe in Dunville
item.dc:creator: Richard Duggan
item.description: Well folks, here's a yarn for ya: over in Dunville, last Friday, the RCMP's got their hands full investigating a right unfortunate mishap. Seems like a backhoe and a moped decided to have a meet-up at the crossroads of Main Road and Greenhouse Road, around the time most are thinkin' about quittin' work for the week. Tragically, the 61-year-old fella on the moped didn't make it after a trip to the hospital in Placentia. The backhoe operator? Didn't get a scratch, lucky bugger. Makes you wonder what was going on in their heads at that intersection, eh? Just another day where something massive flattens something tiny. Remember folks, out here, big machinery rules the roads, and mopeds, well, they're on the endangered species list.
item.content:encoded: Well folks, here's a yarn for ya: over in Dunville, last Friday, the RCMP's got their hands full investigating a right unfortunate mishap. Seems like a backhoe and a moped decided to have a meet-up at the crossroads of Main Road and Greenhouse Road, around the time most are thinkin' about quittin' work for the week. Tragically, the 61-year-old fella on the moped didn't make it after a trip to the hospital in Placentia. The backhoe operator? Didn't get a scratch, lucky bugger. Makes you wonder what was going on in their heads at that intersection, eh? Just another day where something massive flattens something tiny. Remember folks, out here, big machinery rules the roads, and mopeds, well, they're on the endangered species list.

item.title: Man Dead Following Single-Vehicle Crash Near Norris Arm
item.dc:creator: Richard Duggan
item.description:
Well, b'y, it seems there's been a tragic crash on the Trans Canada by Norris Arm. A young fella, only 22, lost his life after the car went off the road. Police said he wasn't wearin' his seatbelt, and he got thrown from the vehicle — didn't make it, sadly. Tsk, reminds ya of the good ol' "click it or ticket" campaigns, but I guess some folks still take their chances. Another person in the car got hurt and went off to the hospital.
The whole ordeal caused the road to be closed for quite a while. You can imagine the rubberneckers makin' the situation worse. They’re still figurin' out what exactly went wrong. Still, a hard reminder to buckle up, me son!
item.content:encoded:
Well, b'y, it seems there's been a tragic crash on the Trans Canada by Norris Arm. A young fella, only 22, lost his life after the car went off the road. Police said he wasn't wearin' his seatbelt, and he got thrown from the vehicle — didn't make it, sadly. Tsk, reminds ya of the good ol' "click it or ticket" campaigns, but I guess some folks still take their chances. Another person in the car got hurt and went off to the hospital.
The whole ordeal caused the road to be closed for quite a while. You can imagine the rubberneckers makin' the situation worse. They’re still figurin' out what exactly went wrong. Still, a hard reminder to buckle up, me son!

item.title: Human Remains Recovered From Water Outside Placentia Bay
item.dc:creator: Richard Duggan
item.description: Well b'ys, there's some serious goings-on down around Placentia Bay. Some poor bugger's remains were found floatin' about in the water, and it’s got the RCMP all involved. The crew on a commercial vessel stumbled onto the remains and brought 'em back to Argentia to hand 'em over to the Placentia cops. Now the Chief Medical Examiner's got the hard job of tryin' to figure out who this johnny was. Seems like there's always somethin' fishy washinn' up with the tide, hey? Let’s hope they get to the bottom of it quick, before some other wild tale starts up.
item.content:encoded: Well b'ys, there's some serious goings-on down around Placentia Bay. Some poor bugger's remains were found floatin' about in the water, and it’s got the RCMP all involved. The crew on a commercial vessel stumbled onto the remains and brought 'em back to Argentia to hand 'em over to the Placentia cops. Now the Chief Medical Examiner's got the hard job of tryin' to figure out who this johnny was. Seems like there's always somethin' fishy washinn' up with the tide, hey? Let’s hope they get to the bottom of it quick, before some other wild tale starts up.

item.title: RNC Investigating Shooting in CBS
item.dc:creator: Richard Duggan
item.description:
Well b'y, over in Conception Bay South, there was a bit of excitement last night. Some fella ended up in the hospital after a shootin’—nothin' too out of the ordinary for a rock the size of Newfie. The police were called down to Fowler’s Road, near Peacekeepers Way, where it seems some bloke decided to play a little too rough with his toy gun.
Now, the RNC's on the lookout for a fellow in his 50s, white but with a dark tan, as if the bayman’s been spending too much time in the sun pretending it's Mexico. He’s about 6 feet tall with a fair bit of build on him. Last seen struttin' around like Halloween came early, wearin’ a black jacket and jeans, topped off with a bike helmet that’s got flames on it—real incognito-like, eh?
He’s zippin’ around on a red dirt bike, using its headlight to pretend he’s on a grand adventure. Anyway, the cops are askin' folks if they've got dash cam footage around that time or if they saw a Honda Civic nearby. If you’ve got anything to say, give the RNC a shout at 729-8000. Let’s hope they catch the bugger so he can’t go shootin’ up the place again. But really, a dirt bike and a flame helmet? Might as well tattoo 'catch me' on his forehead!
item.content:encoded:
Well b'y, over in Conception Bay South, there was a bit of excitement last night. Some fella ended up in the hospital after a shootin’—nothin' too out of the ordinary for a rock the size of Newfie. The police were called down to Fowler’s Road, near Peacekeepers Way, where it seems some bloke decided to play a little too rough with his toy gun.
Now, the RNC's on the lookout for a fellow in his 50s, white but with a dark tan, as if the bayman’s been spending too much time in the sun pretending it's Mexico. He’s about 6 feet tall with a fair bit of build on him. Last seen struttin' around like Halloween came early, wearin’ a black jacket and jeans, topped off with a bike helmet that’s got flames on it—real incognito-like, eh?
He’s zippin’ around on a red dirt bike, using its headlight to pretend he’s on a grand adventure. Anyway, the cops are askin' folks if they've got dash cam footage around that time or if they saw a Honda Civic nearby. If you’ve got anything to say, give the RNC a shout at 729-8000. Let’s hope they catch the bugger so he can’t go shootin’ up the place again. But really, a dirt bike and a flame helmet? Might as well tattoo 'catch me' on his forehead!

item.title: Executive Federal Government Jobs Spike by 42 Per Cent Since 2016: Canadian Taxpayers Federation
item.dc:creator: Richard Duggan
item.description: Well, b'ys, it looks like Trudeau's crew's been busy hiring more of them high-up bureaucrats in Ottawa. The Canadian Taxpayers Federation is blowin' the whistle, sayin' there's been a whopping 42 percent jump in federal executives since 2016. We're talkin' about over 9,000 of 'em in cushy positions now, each rakin' in between $135,000 to $255,000 a year. A good few of 'em are sittin' pretty and pocketin' bonuses of about $18,000 on top of that. Terrazzano is sayin' taxpayers are coughin' up big time for all this. Seems like the government’s growin’ faster than a Newfoundland snowstorm, and that’s sayin’ something! Maybe one day, they'll wrap some budget line in the boatsafe nonsense and sail it where the sun don't shine.
item.content:encoded: Well, b'ys, it looks like Trudeau's crew's been busy hiring more of them high-up bureaucrats in Ottawa. The Canadian Taxpayers Federation is blowin' the whistle, sayin' there's been a whopping 42 percent jump in federal executives since 2016. We're talkin' about over 9,000 of 'em in cushy positions now, each rakin' in between $135,000 to $255,000 a year. A good few of 'em are sittin' pretty and pocketin' bonuses of about $18,000 on top of that. Terrazzano is sayin' taxpayers are coughin' up big time for all this. Seems like the government’s growin’ faster than a Newfoundland snowstorm, and that’s sayin’ something! Maybe one day, they'll wrap some budget line in the boatsafe nonsense and sail it where the sun don't shine.

item.title: Colourful Coral Discovery Leaves Marine Institute Scientists “Speechless”

media:content.media:description: Anemones (round with tentacles) in the foreground are more abundant in some patches of the soft coral garden. Via Memorial University on Flickr.

item.dc:creator: Richard Duggan
item.description:
Oh b'y, you won't believe this one! Some researchers from the Marine Institute were pokin' around off Newfoundland at Funk Island Deep, mindin' their own business, when they stumbled upon a bed of corals explodin' with all sorts of colors—like that time uncle Joe tried paintin' the shed after a few too many swallies. They found it stretchin' out further than you could chuck a dory.
Now, Miss Emmeline Broad, who was workin' the fancy camera, says they hadn't the faintest clue they'd trip over this underwater extravaganza. Probably shocked as a cod out of water, eh? They were just gawkin' at the screen, speechless like when Nan figured out Facebook.
The spot's a marine refuge, so no worries 'bout anyone destroyin' it just yet. It's been under the radar 'cause the area is all hills and bumps, makin' it a right mess for fishin'. That's their best guess anyway—bit like us laughin' when mainlanders try to drive in a snowstorm.
But honestly, it's a fine ol' reminder of how little we know 'bout what's under the waves. Who knew there was a rainbow party goin' on down there all this time, and we're just findin' out now? Leave it to Newfies to literally trip over somethin' amazin' while out for a stroll!
item.content:encoded:
Oh b'y, you won't believe this one! Some researchers from the Marine Institute were pokin' around off Newfoundland at Funk Island Deep, mindin' their own business, when they stumbled upon a bed of corals explodin' with all sorts of colors—like that time uncle Joe tried paintin' the shed after a few too many swallies. They found it stretchin' out further than you could chuck a dory.
Now, Miss Emmeline Broad, who was workin' the fancy camera, says they hadn't the faintest clue they'd trip over this underwater extravaganza. Probably shocked as a cod out of water, eh? They were just gawkin' at the screen, speechless like when Nan figured out Facebook.
The spot's a marine refuge, so no worries 'bout anyone destroyin' it just yet. It's been under the radar 'cause the area is all hills and bumps, makin' it a right mess for fishin'. That's their best guess anyway—bit like us laughin' when mainlanders try to drive in a snowstorm.
But honestly, it's a fine ol' reminder of how little we know 'bout what's under the waves. Who knew there was a rainbow party goin' on down there all this time, and we're just findin' out now? Leave it to Newfies to literally trip over somethin' amazin' while out for a stroll!

item.title: Missing American Tourist Was Guest at Driftwood Inn
item.dc:creator: Sara Strickland
item.description:
Well b'ys, there’s been quite the hullabaloo over in Deer Lake, let me tell ya. That Driftwood Inn, lovely spot it was, went up in flames early Saturday morning, poof gone! Firefighters did their best, bless 'em, but the old place just couldn’t be saved, God love it. Now, the problem is there was an American fella, 77 years old from Kansas, Mr. Eugene Earl Spoon, staying there. He went missing around Friday night and no one's seen him since. Poor guy probably thought he was in for a quiet trip to ol' Newfie, and look what he gets - a right fiery mess.
The Mounties are digging through the ashes as we speak trying to figure out what's what. Now if anyone's happened to see Mr. Spoon or knows anything, the RCMP over in Deer Lake want a natter. Always something, isn’t it? You come for a bit of peace and quiet and end up in a right conundrum. Sure hope they find him safe, but I tell ya, this trip certainly wasn't in the brochure!
item.content:encoded:
Well b'ys, there’s been quite the hullabaloo over in Deer Lake, let me tell ya. That Driftwood Inn, lovely spot it was, went up in flames early Saturday morning, poof gone! Firefighters did their best, bless 'em, but the old place just couldn’t be saved, God love it. Now, the problem is there was an American fella, 77 years old from Kansas, Mr. Eugene Earl Spoon, staying there. He went missing around Friday night and no one's seen him since. Poor guy probably thought he was in for a quiet trip to ol' Newfie, and look what he gets - a right fiery mess.
The Mounties are digging through the ashes as we speak trying to figure out what's what. Now if anyone's happened to see Mr. Spoon or knows anything, the RCMP over in Deer Lake want a natter. Always something, isn’t it? You come for a bit of peace and quiet and end up in a right conundrum. Sure hope they find him safe, but I tell ya, this trip certainly wasn't in the brochure!

item.title: NDP President Urging Province to Join Federal Pharmacare Plan
item.dc:creator: Sara Strickland
item.description: Well b’ys, the big wigs up in Ottawa got their pharmacare plan passed, so they're chirpin' at our province now to jump on board. The NDP president, Mary Shortall, is chompin' at the bit for us to sign up quick, wouldn’t ya know. Apparently, the new deal will cover a bunch of meds and equipment for folks with diabetes, plus contraceptive care – all for free. She’s sayin' we're blowin’ a cool 70 million on diabetes stuff alone, so you’d think we might want a piece of that pie. But hey, that’s government for ya – always in a rush, eh? Just hope they don’t drag their feet too long, or we’ll all be poorer for it—literally and figuratively!
item.content:encoded: Well b’ys, the big wigs up in Ottawa got their pharmacare plan passed, so they're chirpin' at our province now to jump on board. The NDP president, Mary Shortall, is chompin' at the bit for us to sign up quick, wouldn’t ya know. Apparently, the new deal will cover a bunch of meds and equipment for folks with diabetes, plus contraceptive care – all for free. She’s sayin' we're blowin’ a cool 70 million on diabetes stuff alone, so you’d think we might want a piece of that pie. But hey, that’s government for ya – always in a rush, eh? Just hope they don’t drag their feet too long, or we’ll all be poorer for it—literally and figuratively!

item.title: Prime Minister Facing Increased Pressure to Step Aside as House Reopens
item.dc:creator: Sara Strickland
item.description: Well, me ducky, things are heatin' up over in the House of Commons. They're back from a bit of a break, but it's not exactly smooth sailing. There's talk that this might be the last call for Trudeau—some folks are even passin' a note around sayin’ it’s time for him to pack it in. The whole place is all tangled up ‘cause the Conservatives are pushin' for answers on what exactly the government’s been splashin’ the cash on with these fancy green tech projects. Not surprisin', though; politics these days seem about as organized as a crowd at George Street Festival. Anyway, this Wednesday's meeting might be the moment things hit the fan for ol' Justin—if he ever stops hidin' and shows up, that is.
item.content:encoded: Well, me ducky, things are heatin' up over in the House of Commons. They're back from a bit of a break, but it's not exactly smooth sailing. There's talk that this might be the last call for Trudeau—some folks are even passin' a note around sayin’ it’s time for him to pack it in. The whole place is all tangled up ‘cause the Conservatives are pushin' for answers on what exactly the government’s been splashin’ the cash on with these fancy green tech projects. Not surprisin', though; politics these days seem about as organized as a crowd at George Street Festival. Anyway, this Wednesday's meeting might be the moment things hit the fan for ol' Justin—if he ever stops hidin' and shows up, that is.
